01 About me
Designing with purpose & curiosity

Kanak Yadav is a sixth-semester B.Des student at IILM University, Gurugram, studying Product Design and Management. With a strong foundation in user research, UX design, and 3D modelling, she translates insights into functional, user-centred design solutions.

Genuinely interested in fashion, Gen Z culture, and luxury aesthetics — whether designing a digital product, an original brand, or a physical concept.

02 Selected projects
Redesign — hi-fi mockup
Before — original website
Project 01
NewMe — Shop Page Redesign
UI/UX · Web Redesign

NewMe is a Gen Z fast-fashion brand from India. Their shop page had a cluttered, mobile-style layout that broke on desktop. I redesigned it into a clean, structured desktop-first experience with clear hierarchy and better navigation.

What I solved
Rebuilt layout for desktop — away from mobile narrow column
Removed disruptive app download banners
Redesigned product grid for cleaner discovery
Improved navigation and filter system
Established clear typographic hierarchy
FigmaLo-fi wireframesHi-fi prototypeE-commerce
View Figma file →
Project 02
Bloom — Luxury Perfume Brand
Brand Identity · Web Design

Bloom is an original luxury fragrance brand I created from concept to website. Each bottle is inspired by a flower — Rose, Jasmine Lily, Magnolia, and Lily — with its own personality, colour story, and theme.

What I designed
Original brand concept — naming, identity, bottle design
Idea to product — illustrated then refined each bottle
Floral-inspired visual system with soft editorial palette
Homepage, product listings, and detail pages in Figma
FigmaBrand identityWeb designLuxuryOriginal brand
View prototype →
Project 03
Paw Sofa Chair — Product Concept
Product Design · Concept

A furniture concept inspired by playful ergonomics — the shape of an animal paw translated into a functional, character-driven sofa chair. From sketches through material studies to a physical prototype.

Design process
Ideation and hand sketches exploring paw form language
Exploded-view material breakdown — foam, fabric, wood
Technical cross-section drawings with dimensions
Physical prototype to validate ergonomics and form
IdeationSketchingErgonomicsPrototypeFurniture
